title: thymus longicaulis var. moesiacus
intro: Thymus longicaulis var. moesiacus, commonly known as Mountain Thyme, is a subspecies of thyme plant, native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, pink or purple flowers and fragrant leaves. The plant is rich in essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Mountain Thyme have been used to alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to support the immune system, while also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Mountain Thyme is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and protecting against infections.
